name: acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid
summary: Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid is a boswellic acid emphasized in Boswellia standardization discussions. Its relevance is mechanistically strong for inflammatory pathways, but isolated-compound human evidence is much thinner than extract-level evidence.
description: Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid is a boswellic acid emphasized in Boswellia standardization discussions. Its relevance is mechanistically strong for inflammatory pathways, but isolated-compound human evidence is much thinner than extract-level evidence.
safetyNotes: A 90-day trial evaluated a specific Boswellia extract enriched to 30% AKBA with laboratory safety monitoring; that product-level evidence cannot establish the safety of isolated AKBA. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, bleeding, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
safety: Safety evidence: limited human evidence for an AKBA-enriched Boswellia extract, not isolated AKBA. A 90-day trial monitored one extract standardized to 30% AKBA; pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, bleeding, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety for isolated AKBA remain insufficient.
